entries.author: Origen of Alexandria
entries.title: DEUTERONOMY, THE SECOND LAW.
entries.body: And here this other fact will not appear to be without significance, that it is Moses who hears from God all that is written down in the law of Leviticus, whereas in Deuteronomy it is the people who are represented as listening to Moses and learning from him what they could not hear from God.{fn:0} This indeed is why it is called Deuteronomy, meaning the second law. A fact which some will think points to this [is] that when the first law given through Moses{fn:1} came to an end, a second legislation was apparently composed, and this was specially delivered by Moses to his successor Joshua.{fn:2} And Joshua is certainly believed to be a figure of our Savior, by whose second law, that is, by the precepts of the Gospels, all things are brought to perfection. {fn:3}

entries.author: Didache
entries.title: BE JUDICIOUS.
entries.body: Do not desire any schism, but make peace among those who fight. Judge justly, and do not show favor to anyone in correcting offenses. Do not waver whether a thing shall or shall not be. {fn:0}

entries.author: Origen of Alexandria
entries.title: AS A FATHER TEACHES HIS SON.
entries.body: See what we are generally taught about God: God is not as a man to be deceived nor as the son of man to be threatened,{fn:0} and we learn that God is not as man. But other texts say that God is as a man: For the Lord your God has taught you as a man teaches his son,{fn:1} and again, As a man he takes on the manners of his son. Hence, wherever the Scriptures speak theologically about God in relation to himself and do not involve his plan for human matters, they teach that he is not as a man.{fn:2} For there will be no limit to his greatness,{fn:3} and he is more feared than all of the gods,{fn:4} and praise him, all you angels of God; praise him, all his hosts; praise him, sun and moon; praise him, all stars and light.{fn:5} You can find many other passages in the sacred Scriptures to which you can relate the words God is not as a man. {fn:6} See
